subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Platelets: The Tiny Heroes
description: A quick, practical guide to Platelets: The Tiny Heroes.

hook.text: Platelets are the smallest cells in your blood—and the first to show up at a wound.

buildup.text: Your blood contains about 250,000 platelets per microliter—billions in your whole body.

discovery.text: When activated, platelets change shape from discs to spiny spheres that grip each other.

twist.text: Platelets live only 8-10 days. Your body produces 100 billion new ones every single day.

climax.text: Low platelet counts—thrombocytopenia—make even small injuries dangerously hard to stop.

punchline.text: Tiny, short-lived, and absolutely essential. Thank your platelets.

quiz.question: How long does a platelet typically live?
